name: Gluconeogenesis
description: Gluconeogenesis: pyruvate is converted to glucose by bypassing the three irreversible glycolytic steps (pyruvate carboxylase/PEPCK, fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase, glucose-6-phosphatase), gated by energy charge and reciprocal hormonal regulation in liver.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Pyruvate"]
    N2["Pyruvate carboxylase -> OAA"]
    N3["PEPCK -> PEP"]
    N4["Climb reverse glycolysis"]
    N5["Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase"]
    N6{"Energy charge adequate?"}
    N7["F6P -> G6P"]
    N8["Glucose-6-phosphatase (ER)"]
    N9["Glucose exported"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|yes| N7
    N6 -->|low ATP: stall| N5
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

nodeDetails.label: Energy charge adequate?
nodeDetails.detail: ATP and substrate availability gate flux

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

sources.title: Molecular physiology of the regulation of hepatic gluconeogenesis and glycolysis

sources.title: Gluconeogenesis and related aspects of glycolysis
